title: synthyris missurica subsp. stellata
intro: Synthyris missurica subsp. stellata, also known as the stellate bog girolle or stellate synthyris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family, native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. The health benefits of Synthyris missurica subsp. stellata include its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds, burns, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots and rhizomes to make a tincture or decoction.
